title: Bareta Gas Service Budklada Road, ... vs Ash. Sishan Kumar on 4 March, 2014
body: Bareta Gas Service Budklada Road, ... vs Ash. Sishan Kumar on 4 March, 2014 2nd Additional Bench STATE CONSUMER DISPUTES REDRESSAL COMMISSION, PUNJAB DAKSHIN MARG, SECTOR 37-A, CHANDIGARH Misc. Application No. 277 of 2014 in First Appeal No. 1177 of 2011 Date of institution: 18.2.2014 Date of Decision: 4.3.2014 Bareta Gas Service Budklada Road, Bareta, District Mansa through its Manager Ashwani Kumar. .....Applicant/Appellant/OP Versus Ash. Sishan Kumar son of Sh. Om Parkash, Resident of Kishan Garh (Medha Singh Wala) Tehsil Budhlada, District Mansa. .....Respondent/Complainant Argued By:- For the applicant/appellant : Ms. Maninderpreet Kaur, Adv. for Sh. Kamal Sateja, Adv. Application U/ Order 9, Rule 9 CPC for restoration of the appeal. Quorum:- Shri Gurcharan Singh Saran, Presiding Judicial Member Shri Vinod Kumar Gupta, Member Shri Harcharan Singh Guram, Member ORDER Gurcharan Singh Saran, Presiding Judicial Member Applicant-appellant(hereinafter called 'the appellant') has filed the present application under Order 9, Rule 9 CPC for restoration of the appeal, dismissed for want of prosecution vide order dated 19.8.2013 of the Commission. Misc. Application No. 277 of 2014 2 In First Appeal No. 1177 of 2014 2. We have gone through the contents of the application, stating that the date was wrongly noted by the Clerk of the counsel, therefore, they could not appear before the Commission. However, they have not been able to explain that they also did not appear on 14.9.2012, 15.2.2013 and 15.4.2013 and ultimately, when none appeared on 19.8.2013, the complaint was dismissed in default under Section 22A of the C.P.Act. 3. In view of Section 22A of the Consumer Protection Act, 1986 (in short 'the Act'), the power of review or recall has vested with the National Commission only. There is no provision under the Act which provides for recalling or reviewing its own orders by the State Commission. As there was divergence of views by the two decisions i.e. " Jyotsana Arvind Kumar Shah Vs Bombay Hospital Trust", JT 1999(5)-228" and " New India Assurance Company Limited Vs S. Srinivasan ", 2000(1)CLT-414(SC)" and the Hon'ble Supreme Court after discussing both the judgments held in case " Rajeev Hitendra Pathak & Ors. Vs Achyut Kashinath Karekar & Anr. ", 2011 (4) RCR (Civil)-175 and has settled the law once for all that the State Commission has no power to recall or review the orders passed by it, by observing in para-38 as follows:- "In our considered opinion, the decision in Jyotsana 's case laid down the correct law and the view taken in the later decision of this Court in New India Assurance Co. Ltd. is untenable and cannot be sustained". 4. In view of the above Section as well as settled proposition/law, this Commission does not have any power to recall its order. In case the applicant is aggrieved with the order so passed by this Commission, he should go for appeal/revision petition before Misc. Application No. 277 of 2014 3 In First Appeal No. 1177 of 2014 the Hon'ble National Commission against this order. Therefore, we are of the opinion that the application is not maintainable before this Commission and the same is hereby dismissed. 5. The arguments in this application were heard on 28.2.2014 and the order was reserved. Now the order be communicated to the parties as per rules. (Gurcharan Singh Saran) Presiding Judicial Member (Vinod Kumar Gupta) Member March 4, 2014. (Harcharan Singh Guram) as Member
